text: Since its inception in 1951, the k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm has evolved into a cornerstone of modern data mining, recommendation engines, and Internet of Things (IoT) applications. This paper provides a comprehensive review and performance analysis of advancements in exact kNN techniques, with a specific focus on kNN Search and kNN Join operations within high-dimensional spaces. We conduct a deep dive into 31 search methods and 12 join methods, offering a methodological overview and critical insights into their respective strengths, limitations, and practical utility. To support reproducibility and further experimentation, we provide the source code for every method discussed. Driven by the increasing complexity of high-dimensional data and the lack of exhaustive surveys on exact kNN approaches, this work seeks to fill a critical gap in the literature. Furthermore, we identify persistent challenges and propose future research directions, providing a holistic guide that synthesizes, compares, and evaluates the current state of the art in kNN methodology.
